name: Threads Post: PLDT solar bundle / duopoly extends into power (lunch break re-run)
markdown_description:
## Hook
PLDT now wants to bundle internet with solar power. Multi-year subscription. Multi-year lock-in.

## Full Threads post (publish text)
PLDT now wants to bundle internet with solar power. Multi-year subscription. Multi-year lock-in.

In NZ, around 30 ISPs compete on a regulated fibre wholesale layer. Same network, different sellers, real choice.

In the Philippines, two companies already control fixed broadband. Now one of them wants to own your roof too.

It's not perfect, no country is. But the floor is built different.

In one country your provider competes for you. In the other your provider corners you.

## Pillar
Pillar 4 (Economic & Utility Reform) topic 4.5, with secondary anchor in Pillar 1 (Governance Comparison) topic 1.13.

## Type
Reactive (news anchor: PLDT Home announcement 2026-05-28 of an internet + solar power bundle for households).

- Post: output\posts\2026-05-29-pldt-solar-bundle-duopoly-threads-lunch-break.md

## Scheduled Publish
2026-05-29 12:30 PHT (Asia/Manila)
epoch_ms: 1780029000000

## Status note
Fully automated; no manual review required by default. Changing the status to NEEDS-REVISION before publish will halt the publisher daemon for this task.

## Re-run differentiation
This run differs from the earlier 12:30 PM Lunch Break post for today (Meralco P3 vs Entrust P13,000, dividend math). Same pillar, structurally different argument: market structure / duopoly extending lock-in surface from broadband into household energy. The reader who already saw the earlier post gets a fresh angle on Pillar 4, not a repeat.

## Voice + style check
- Third-person voice throughout. Zero first-person pronouns. Collective framing only.
- Zero em dashes anywhere.
- Tagalog deliberately omitted this run; the English parallel-structure verdict was sharper than any short Tagalog phrase tested for this argument. Per brand-context.md Section 4.
- NZ caveat ('It's not perfect, no country is.') present verbatim immediately after the NZ praise.
- Character count: 479 (under the 500 Threads ideal).
- No individual politicians or executives named. Corporations only.
